mission.title: Install and Configure Kuasar on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuasar is a multi-sandbox container runtime that provides cloud-native solutions for various container scenarios. Installing Kuasar allows you to leverage its efficient container management capabilities, enhancing your cloud-native applications.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for Kuasar to operate in. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace kuasar
```

steps.title: Deploy Kuasar Components
steps.description:
Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Kuasar. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-deployment.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-service.yaml -n kuasar
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Kuasar pods are running correctly by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuasar
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kuasar will have the pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080 on your local machine. Additionally, resource limits will be configured for optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-deployment.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-service.yaml -n kuasar

uninstall.title: Remove Kuasar Components
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Kuasar, delete the resources you created:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-deployment.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-service.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl delete namespace kuasar
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kuasar
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Kuasar, first delete the existing deployment and then apply the new manifests:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-deployment.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.0.0/deploy/kuasar-service.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.1.0/deploy/kuasar-deployment.yaml -n kuasar
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/v1.1.0/deploy/kuasar-service.yaml -n kuasar
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kuasar pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuasar
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not accessible, ensure that the service type is correctly set (e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort) and check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kuasar
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to resource allocation, verify that your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) available:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your local machine.
